comment.body: Well bears have a certain narrative going for them but the GOV is spending close to $170B a month from the TGA. This is not QE, but it’s effect on the economy is very QE like until the TGA has to be refilled. There is plenty of fire power to cause a screaming rally. Also QT has dropped by $25B, small beer in comparison but notable. I would be watching for signs of bottoming

comment.body: Yep, but that took 9 months and folks were convinced we were going to have a recession as rates ramped up and inflation was running at 9%. However I am not sanguine. I have no clue were we are headed, I just think if we dump Monday this market is really oversold. I also think there is a lot of money slopping around as TGA is spent, usually that money finds its way into asset prices and holds rates down… that not usually an environment for a crash I think the greater probability is for a big rally sometime this week.
